pi_items.source: Standard 1-bit full adder implementation verified against GeeksforGeeks, TutorialsPoint, and electronics-tutorials.ws. Formula Sum = (A XOR B) XOR Cin requires exactly 2 XOR gates.
Standard 1-bit full adder implementation verified against GeeksforGeeks, TutorialsPoint, and electronics-tutorials.ws. Formula Cout = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B)) requires exactly 2 AND gates.
Standard 1-bit full adder implementation verified against GeeksforGeeks, TutorialsPoint, and electronics-tutorials.ws. Formula Cout = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B)) requires exactly 1 OR gate.

Standard 1-bit full adder I/O naming conventions verified against multiple authoritative sources (GeeksforGeeks, TutorialsPoint, electronics-tutorials.ws). A and B are input bits, Cin is carry-in, Sum is sum output, Cout is carry-out output.
Mathematically verified against complete truth table provided in setup_task.sh. All 8 truth table entries (000 through 111) produce correct Sum values according to formula.

pi_items.metadata_value: (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))
pi_items.verified_value: (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))
pi_items.source: Mathematically verified against complete truth table provided in setup_task.sh. All 8 truth table entries (000 through 111) produce correct Cout values according to formula.
Implemented both formulas in Python and verified each of the 8 truth table rows (A,B,Cin combinations). All outputs match expected values.
Verified against digital logic textbooks and authoritative sources (GeeksforGeeks, TutorialsPoint). Two half-adders with an OR gate is a standard topology for implementing a full adder.
Code inspection: setup_task.sh lines 8-40 create the full_adder_spec.txt file with all content inline using shell here-document.

privileged_info_summary: A 1-bit full adder requires exactly 2 XOR gates, 2 AND gates, and 1 OR gate when implemented using the standard two half-adders topology. The Boolean equations are: Sum = (A XOR B) XOR Cin and Cout = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B)). These equations are mathematically correct, as verified against the complete truth table of all 8 input combinations (A, B, Cin ∈ {0,1}). The required I/O labels are the standard full adder signal names: inputs A, B, Cin and outputs Sum, Cout.
